description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves are deep and dark, contrasting with the speckled, granite-like texture of the stone's upper surface. The sides of the block are solid and dark, lacking the grooved pattern seen on the top. The coloration of the stone is mottled, with shades of gray, black, and hints of brown and purple, suggesting a composite or aggregate stone material. The block is thick and robust, likely designed to serve as a heavy-duty barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. Its overall appearance is utilitarian, with a focus on durability and resistance to wear.
description: A thick, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface and a mottled, granite-like texture. The sides are solid and dark, emphasizing its robust, barrier-like design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the grooved top surface and the solid, dark front face of the block.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the solid side face visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the grooved top and solid back face.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the deep grooves and the solid, dark side face.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a protective barrier or decorative obstacle
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the surface appears clean and well-maintained, with no moss or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or spaced as a standalone barrier
